name: Wat Arun
subtext: Temple of Dawn on the Chao Phraya riverside
about: One of Bangkok's most iconic landmarks, Wat Arun sits majestically on the west bank of the Chao Phraya River. The temple's spire rises 70 meters, decorated with colorful porcelain and seashells that glitter in the sunlight.
notes: Go at sunset for the best photos from across the river
Entry fee: 100 THB per person
Wear clothes covering shoulders and knees
Take the ferry from Tha Tien pier — only 4 THB

name: May Veggie Home
subtext: Best vegetarian Thai food in Silom area
about: A beloved vegetarian restaurant serving authentic Thai dishes made entirely from plant-based ingredients. The kitchen recreates classic Thai flavors without any meat, earning rave reviews from vegans and omnivores alike.
notes: Fully vegetarian with vegan options clearly marked
Try the Pad Thai and Green Curry
Can get crowded at lunch, go before 11:30 AM
Very affordable — most dishes under 150 THB

name: Rot Fai Market
subtext: Massive night market with vintage finds and street food
about: One of Bangkok's best-kept secrets, the Train Night Market is a sprawling bazaar filled with vintage clothing, retro furniture, handmade goods, and some of the city's tastiest street food.
notes: Open only Thursday to Sunday evenings
Much less touristy than Chatuchak
Vintage section has amazing retro finds
Multiple vegetarian stalls near main entrance

name: Sky Bar at Lebua
subtext: Iconic rooftop bar from The Hangover Part II
about: Perched 63 floors above the streets of Bangkok, this open-air rooftop bar offers unmatched panoramic views of the city skyline and the Chao Phraya River. Made famous by The Hangover Part II.
notes: Strict dress code — no shorts, sandals, or sleeveless
Go right at 5 PM for best seats without reservation
Cocktails start around 600+ THB
Sunset views are absolutely unreal

name: Ethos Vegetarian
subtext: Chill backpacker-style veggie cafe near Khao San
about: A relaxed haven for vegetarians in the Khao San area. Multiple floors with floor seating upstairs, serving a mix of Thai, Indian and Western vegetarian dishes at backpacker-friendly prices.
notes: 100% vegetarian with great vegan options
Relaxed floor seating area upstairs
Thai, Indian, and Western options
Smoothie bowls are a must-try

name: Chatuchak Market
subtext: World's largest outdoor market — 15,000+ stalls
about: A shopper's paradise spanning 35 acres with over 15,000 stalls selling everything from vintage clothing and handmade jewelry to home decor and exotic plants.
notes: Open only Saturday and Sunday
Go early morning to beat crowds and heat
Sections 2-4 for best clothing deals
Coconut ice cream stalls everywhere

name: Phra Arthit Alley
subtext: Quiet lane with street art, old shophouses and local cafes
about: A charming hidden alley near the river filled with colorful street art, century-old shophouses converted into indie cafes, and a peaceful atmosphere far from the tourist chaos.
notes: Walk from Khao San towards the river
Best explored on foot in late afternoon
Several cat cafes hidden in the side lanes
Great for photos — very Instagrammable

name: Illuzion Nightclub
subtext: DJ Mag #9 mega-club with 3 rooms and 5,000 capacity
about: Phuket's biggest nightclub sits at the heart of Bangla Road. Three rooms — Main EDM, Empire hip-hop, and Shelter techno — cater to every taste. The 5,000-person capacity venue was ranked #9 globally by DJ Mag in 2025.
notes: Skip the 800 THB open-bar deal — drinks are watered down
Negotiate VIP in person for better rates
Smart casual dress code, no beachwear
Friday is hip-hop night, Saturday is peak EDM

name: Sugar Club
subtext: Best hip-hop & R&B club in Phuket, 2,000 capacity
about: Opposite Illuzion on the 3rd floor, Sugar Club is Phuket's premier hip-hop, R&B, and Afrobeats destination. With a 350 sqm LED setup and professional dancers, it took over the space after Seduction permanently closed.
notes: Don't show up before 11 PM — it's dead early
Fills to capacity after midnight every night
800-1,500 THB budget per person
Best hip-hop club in Phuket, no contest

name: Tiger Bar Complex
subtext: Multi-level beer bars + disco with giant tiger facade
about: The iconic giant concrete tigers mark the top of Bangla Road. Ground floor is a collection of lively beer bars, while the upper level transforms into a disco. Their 599 THB open bar offers unlimited local drinks until 2 AM.
notes: Start here with the open bar, then move to Illuzion or Sugar
Keep your glass for refills — saves hassle
599 THB open bar is actually decent for local drinks
Good for groups wanting a bar-hopping warm-up

name: BarFunk
subtext: European-designed club/bar hybrid with chandeliers
about: High ceilings, chandeliers, and European design set BarFunk apart from the neon chaos of Bangla Road. A stylish warm-up venue that bridges the gap between cocktail bar and nightclub.
notes: Great warm-up venue before the mega-clubs
They sell branded merch — quirky Phuket souvenirs
600-1,200 THB budget per person
Best on Friday and Saturday nights

name: New York Bar
subtext: Best live music on Bangla Road — 3 bands nightly
about: Tucked in Soi Viking off the main Bangla strip, New York Bar is where the live music lovers go. Three bands rotate nightly with excellent sound in an air-conditioned venue — a refreshing change from the thumping EDM.
notes: Get there by 9 PM for stage-side seats
Live bands every single night
400-800 THB budget — much cheaper than clubs
Great for couples who want music without the club chaos

name: Monsoon Bar
subtext: Live music + pool tables until 4 AM
about: A solid live music bar on the main Bangla strip with a genuinely talented house band and nightly drink specials. The real draw? Pool tables upstairs that stay open until 4 AM — the best late-night wind-down on Bangla.
notes: Pool tables upstairs until 4 AM — best late-night plan
House band is genuinely talented
Nightly drink specials worth asking about
Burgers and nachos if you need fuel

name: Catch Beach Club
subtext: The gold standard of Phuket beach clubs since 2008
about: Phuket's original premium beach club. Infinity pool, house DJs at sunset, and Mediterranean dining on Bang Tao Beach. Friday BBQ (7-10 PM) and Sunday Brunch (12-3 PM) are legendary events.
notes: Book sunset slot (4-6 PM) — it sells out
Reservations essential Dec-Mar high season
Friday BBQ and Sunday Brunch are the flagship events
3,000-5,000 THB budget per person

name: Cafe Del Mar
subtext: Real Ibiza vibes — ARTBAT played here Jan 2026
about: The authentic Ibiza-branded beach club on Kamala Beach. Pool and beach seating that gets LOUD after 4 PM. The Club Room operates Wednesday-Sunday from 10 PM during high season, hosting international DJs.
notes: Book loungers in advance during high season
Pool beds get shade earlier than beach ones
Walk-ins OK for drinks and dinner
Dennis Cruz confirmed for Songkran 2026

name: XANA Beach Club
subtext: Luxury 35m swim-up pool without the chaos
about: Part of the Angsana Laguna resort on Bang Tao, XANA offers a 35-meter swim-up pool and clean white decor. It's not a party venue — and that's exactly the appeal for couples wanting beach club vibes without pretension.
notes: Visit Friday for the seafood BBQ buffet
Great for couples wanting beach club without pretension
1,500-3,000 THB budget per person
Thai, European, and Mediterranean food options

name: Carpe Diem Beach Club
subtext: BEST pool party in Phuket — Sunday is the day
about: Home to Phuket's best pool party. Every Sunday features international DJs, performers, and a spectacular fire show at 7 PM. The party builds from mid-afternoon to a sunset crescendo. Wednesday is 'Chill & Splash' for a mellower vibe.
notes: Sunday pool party is the flagship — don't miss it
Arrive early for poolside spots
La Dolce Vita Brunch ~3,745 THB
Fire show at 7 PM every Sunday

name: RAVA Beach Club
subtext: Brand new luxury club — 3 infinity pools, 150m beachfront
about: Opened in 2024/25 and backed by the Banyan Tree group, RAVA boasts three infinity pools and one of the longest beachfronts in Thailand at 150 meters. New means fewer crowds and more attentive service.
notes: Brand new — fewer crowds, better service
Worth checking before word spreads
3,000-6,000 THB budget per person
Three infinity pools facing the Andaman Sea

name: Baba Nest
subtext: Arguably the world's most beautiful rooftop bar
about: Perched atop Sri Panwa resort on Cape Panwa, Baba Nest offers a jaw-dropping 360° Andaman panorama with an infinity pool. Open only 5-8 PM, it's one of the most exclusive sunset experiences on Earth.
notes: 2-WEEK WAITLIST in high season — book 1 month ahead
Only 3 hours, held only 15 min — don't be late
3,000 THB minimum spend per person
Extension costs 2,000 THB minimum

name: KEE Sky Lounge
subtext: Best rooftop in Patong with jacuzzi and ocean views
about: Atop the Kee Resort, this rooftop bar spans two floors — a 6th-floor bar and 7th-floor sunbeds with a rooftop jacuzzi facing the ocean. Relaxed, no pretension, and the perfect pre-Bangla sunset warm-up.
notes: Hit at 5:30-6:30 PM for golden hour
Walk to Bangla Road after — perfect pre-party
800-1,500 THB budget per person
Rooftop jacuzzi on 7F is the highlight

name: The Fool Speakeasy
subtext: #1 Best Bar in Phuket 2025 — tarot-themed cocktails
about: Draw a tarot card and receive a custom cocktail with a full reading at Phuket's #1 bar (ranked #20 in Thailand). Set in a beautifully restored 3-storey building in Old Town, it's a one-of-a-kind experience.
notes: The 'Death' cocktail is the crowd favorite
Arrive early or expect a wait
Upstairs red banquettes are more secluded for couples
600-1,200 THB budget per person

name: Estrela Rooftop Bar
subtext: 360° views over Old Town from the 17th floor
about: Perched on the 17th floor of the Novotel in Old Town, Estrela offers 360° views over the historic streets, surrounding hills, and Chalong Bay. Good cocktails, proper food, and free parking.
notes: Ask about 'Dine in the Sky' set menus
Pair with a pre-dinner Old Town walk
1,000-2,000 THB budget per person
Free parking — rare for a rooftop bar

name: Walking Street
subtext: Pattaya's legendary neon-lit nightlife strip
about: A one-kilometer stretch that transforms into a pedestrian-only zone every evening. Wall-to-wall clubs, go-go bars, live music venues, street performers, and cheap eats — Walking Street is Southeast Asia's most famous nightlife strip.
notes: Goes pedestrian-only from 6 PM — arrive early to explore sober
Keep your wallet in your front pocket
Most clubs have no cover charge but drink prices vary wildly
Seafood stalls at the far end are surprisingly good

name: Sanctuary of Truth
subtext: Mind-blowing all-wood temple — no nails, all carvings
about: A 105-meter tall temple made entirely from teak wood without a single nail. Every inch is hand-carved with intricate religious and mythological figures from Thai, Khmer, Chinese, and Indian traditions. Still under construction after 40+ years.
notes: 500 THB entry — worth every baht
Wear a hard hat (provided) inside — still under construction
Best photos from the beach side at golden hour
Dolphin show and horse riding included in the ticket

name: Jomtien Night Market
subtext: Locals' night market — way better than tourist traps
about: While tourists flock to the Pattaya Floating Market, locals eat at Jomtien Night Market. Fresh seafood BBQ, papaya salad, Thai desserts, and cheap clothes — all at local prices with zero tourist markup.
notes: Way cheaper and more authentic than touristy Pattaya markets
Grilled seafood skewers — 40-80 THB each
Thai iced tea here is the real deal
Best on weekends when more vendors show up

name: Pattaya Viewpoint (Pratumnak Hill)
subtext: Best panoramic view of Pattaya Bay — free entry
about: A hilltop viewpoint between Pattaya and Jomtien offering stunning panoramic views of the entire bay, the city skyline, and Koh Larn island. Home to a golden Buddha statue and refreshingly uncrowded compared to the chaos below.
notes: Sunset from here is incredible — arrive by 5:30 PM
Free entry, free parking
Monkeys roam freely — don't carry open food
Combine with a visit to Big Buddha right next door

name: Koh Larn (Coral Island)
subtext: Crystal-clear water just 45 min from Pattaya — day trip essential
about: Pattaya's beach water is murky, but a short 45-minute ferry ride brings you to Koh Larn — an island with six white-sand beaches and turquoise water that rivals anything in southern Thailand. The ultimate Pattaya day trip.
notes: Public ferry 30 THB one-way from Bali Hai Pier — leaves every hour
Rent a scooter on the island — 300 THB/day
Tien Beach is the most beautiful and least crowded
Last ferry back at 6:30 PM — don't miss it

name: Horizon Rooftop Bar
subtext: 34th floor sunset cocktails overlooking the bay
about: Perched atop the Hilton Pattaya, Horizon is one of the classiest rooftop bars on the Eastern Seaboard. Floor-to-ceiling views of the bay, Koh Larn in the distance, and expertly crafted cocktails — without the Bangla Road chaos.
notes: Smart casual dress code — no flip-flops
Happy hour 5-7 PM has great deals
Book a window seat for sunset — fills up fast
800-1,500 THB budget per person

name: Tree Tops Sky Dining & Bar
subtext: Hidden treehouse bar in a garden — Pattaya's best-kept secret
about: Tucked away from the chaos in a lush garden, Tree Tops is an elevated wooden treehouse bar serving craft cocktails and fusion food. Fairy lights, greenery, and live acoustic music create an atmosphere that feels nothing like Pattaya.
notes: Doesn't feel like Pattaya at all — in the best way
Live acoustic music on weekends
Signature cocktails are genuinely creative
Mosquito repellent recommended — it's garden dining

name: Railay Beach
subtext: Accessible only by boat — Thailand's most dramatic cliffs
about: Surrounded by sheer limestone cliffs, Railay is a peninsula only reachable by longtail boat. Four beaches, world-class rock climbing, a hidden lagoon, and some of Thailand's most jaw-dropping scenery. This is what postcards are made of.
notes: Longtail from Ao Nang — 100 THB one-way, 15 minutes
Phra Nang Beach (south) is the most beautiful
Hike to the viewpoint for insane panoramas — 30 min, steep
Stay overnight to experience it without the day-trip crowds

name: Tiger Cave Temple (Wat Tham Suea)
subtext: 1,237 steps to the top — brutal climb, godly views
about: A sacred Buddhist temple set into a limestone cave, with a challenging 1,237-step staircase leading to a golden Buddha at the summit. The 360° panoramic view of Krabi from the top is one of the best in all of Thailand.
notes: Start before 9 AM to avoid the heat — it's brutal
Bring water — there's nothing at the top
Wear proper shoes, not flip-flops
Watch out for monkeys stealing your stuff at the base

name: Krabi Night Market
subtext: Best street food in Krabi — locals and tourists side by side
about: Every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday evening, Krabi Town's waterfront transforms into a vibrant night market. Thai street food, live music, handmade crafts, and a genuinely local vibe that tourist Ao Nang can't match.
notes: Friday-Sunday only — don't show up on weekdays
Roti lady near the entrance is legendary
Grilled seafood section in the back — super fresh
Most dishes 40-120 THB — very affordable

name: Emerald Pool & Blue Pool
subtext: Crystal-clear natural pools hidden in the jungle
about: Fed by a hot spring, the Emerald Pool is a stunning turquoise natural pool in the middle of the Krabi rainforest. A short jungle hike beyond leads to the Blue Pool — an impossibly vivid electric-blue sinkhole that looks photoshopped.
notes: 200 THB entry fee for foreigners
Go before 10 AM — tour buses arrive from 10:30
Swimming allowed in Emerald Pool, NOT in Blue Pool
Boardwalk is slippery — wear shoes with grip

name: Four Islands Tour
subtext: Longtail boat tour — 4 islands in one day
about: The classic Krabi island-hopping day trip covers Tup Island (with its sand bridge), Chicken Island (named for the rock formation), Poda Island (postcard-perfect beach), and Phra Nang Cave Beach. Best done by private longtail for flexibility.
location: Departs from Ao Nang / Nopparat Thara
notes: Private longtail ~2,500-3,000 THB for the whole boat
Group tours are cheaper (~800 THB/person) but rushed
Tup Island sandbar only visible at low tide — check tides
Bring reef-safe sunscreen and snorkeling gear

name: Lae Lay Grill
subtext: Cliffside seafood restaurant with the best sunset in Krabi
about: Built into the cliffside overlooking Ao Nang bay, Lae Lay Grill is where you go for special-occasion seafood with the most spectacular sunset views in Krabi. Fresh catches displayed on ice at the entrance — you pick, they grill.
notes: Book the sunset terrace by 4 PM — it WILL be full otherwise
Seafood by weight — check prices before ordering
Budget 800-2,000 THB per person for a proper meal
Vegetarian options are limited — mostly stir-fries

name: Surat Thani Night Market
subtext: Authentic southern Thai street food — zero tourists
about: While most travelers just pass through Surat Thani, the nightly street market near the TAT office is a goldmine of authentic southern Thai food. Fiery curries, roti mataba, fresh seafood, and local desserts — all at rock-bottom prices.
notes: Southern Thai food is SPICY — ask for 'mai pet' if you can't handle it
Roti mataba stalls are the highlight — 30-50 THB each
Zero English menus — use Google Translate camera
Great way to kill time if you're catching a morning ferry

name: Khao Sok Day Trip
subtext: Ancient rainforest + limestone lake — Thailand's hidden Jurassic Park
about: Khao Sok National Park is a 160-million-year-old rainforest — older than the Amazon. The star attraction is Cheow Lan Lake, a stunning reservoir surrounded by towering karst limestone formations. Day trips from Surat Thani are easy and unforgettable.
location: Khao Sok, 60 km from Surat Thani
notes: Book the floating bungalow overnight experience if you can
Day trips start from ~1,500 THB including transport and lunch
Morning mist on Cheow Lan Lake is unreal — stay overnight
Leeches in the jungle during rainy season — wear long socks

name: Donsak Pier Seafood Row
subtext: Freshest seafood in Thailand — straight off the boats
about: The row of open-air seafood restaurants near Donsak Pier (the ferry departure point for Koh Samui and Koh Phangan) serves fish that was literally swimming hours ago. Dirt cheap, absolutely fresh, and a perfect pre-ferry meal.
notes: Arrive 1 hour before your ferry for a proper meal
Point at the live fish tanks to order — no menu needed
Whole grilled fish with lime sauce — 150-300 THB
Cash only at most stalls

name: Monkey Training Centre
subtext: Watch coconut-picking monkeys in action — only in Surat Thani
about: A unique cultural experience where trained pigtail macaques demonstrate the traditional art of coconut harvesting. The monkeys climb 20-meter palms, select ripe coconuts, and twist them down. An old Thai tradition that's dying out.
notes: 200 THB entry — includes a coconut to drink
Monkeys are well-treated — this isn't a tourist trap show
30-minute drive from Surat Thani town
Unique experience you won't find anywhere else in Thailand

name: Sunrise Beach
subtext: The main beach — turquoise water, powder sand, zero noise
about: Koh Lipe's longest beach faces east, catching stunning sunrises over the Andaman Sea. The water is shallow, warm, and impossibly clear. Longtail boats bob along the shore, beach bars serve cold Changs, and the pace of life slows to zero.
notes: Set your alarm for sunrise — 6:15 AM, worth every lost minute of sleep
Northern end is quieter, southern end has more bars
Snorkel right off the beach — coral starts 20 meters out
Beach bars serve food till late — no need to go to Walking Street

name: Koh Rokroy Snorkeling
subtext: The best coral reef near Koh Lipe — Nemo included
about: A tiny uninhabited island 15 minutes by longtail from Koh Lipe with some of the healthiest coral reefs in the Andaman Sea. Crystal visibility, clownfish in their anemones, sea turtles if you're lucky. Bring your own snorkel or rent on Lipe.
notes: Longtail boat ~500 THB round trip for 2 people
National park fee 200 THB (foreigners) — pay at the ranger booth
Reef-safe sunscreen ONLY — the coral here is precious
Go in the morning for calmest water and best visibility

name: Walking Street
subtext: Koh Lipe's tiny main drag — barefoot dining and live reggae
about: A narrow sandy lane connecting Sunrise and Pattaya beaches, lined with restaurants, dive shops, bars, and souvenir stalls. No cars, no motorbikes — just barefoot tourists strolling between cocktails and pad thai. The island's social hub after dark.
notes: Papaya Mama has the best pad thai on the island
Prices are 30-50% higher than mainland — it's island tax
ATMs charge 220 THB fee — bring cash from the mainland
Live reggae bars come alive after 9 PM

name: Karma Beach Bar
subtext: Sunset cocktails on Sunset Beach — exactly as it sounds
about: Koh Lipe's west coast faces the sunset, and Karma Beach Bar is the best seat in the house. Beanbags on the sand, cocktails in coconuts, and a nightly sunset show over the Andaman that makes you question your life choices back home.
notes: Arrive by 5 PM to grab a beanbag for sunset
Cocktails 200-350 THB — fair for the island
Fire show some nights — check the board at Walking Street
The path from Walking Street is dark at night — bring your phone torch

name: Koh Adang Viewpoint
subtext: Hike to jaw-dropping views of Koh Lipe from above
about: Take a longtail to neighboring Koh Adang and hike 40 minutes to Chado Cliff viewpoint for a bird's-eye panorama of Koh Lipe, the surrounding islands, and endless Andaman blue. One of the best viewpoints in southern Thailand.
notes: Longtail to Koh Adang — 100 THB from Sunrise Beach
40-minute uphill hike — bring 1L of water minimum
National park fee applies if you haven't already paid
The view of Koh Lipe from above is unbelievable

name: Fisherman's Village
subtext: Friday night market + charming old-town strip
about: Bophut's old fishing village is a charming strip of converted Chinese shophouses now housing boutique stores, restaurants, and art galleries. Every Friday it transforms into a massive night market with live music, street food, and fire shows.
timings: Shops: 10 AM - 10 PM; Friday Market: 5 PM - 11 PM
notes: Friday night market is the MUST — plan your week around it
Fire dancers perform at the beach end after 9 PM
Coco Tam's beanbags on the beach are perfect for sunset
Walk the whole strip — it's only 500 meters

name: Nikki Beach Club
subtext: World-famous luxury beach club — Sunday brunch is legendary
about: The global beach club brand's Samui outpost on Lipa Noi beach. Infinity pool overlooking the gulf, international DJs, and the legendary Sunday Brunch (12-4 PM) that's become Samui's most exclusive weekly event.
notes: Sunday Amazing Brunch is the flagship — book in advance
Pool beds from 1,500 THB minimum spend
Dress code: resort chic, no sports gear
3,000-6,000 THB budget per person

name: Ang Thong Marine Park
subtext: 42 islands, emerald lagoon — the real-life 'The Beach'
about: A protected archipelago of 42 islands with emerald lagoons, white-sand beaches, and jungle-covered limestone peaks. The internal saltwater Emerald Lake (Thale Nai) inspired Alex Garland's novel 'The Beach'. Best experienced as a full-day boat trip.
notes: Closed Nov-Dec during monsoon — check before booking
Speedboat tours (~2,500 THB) beat slow boats for time at islands
Hike to the Emerald Lake viewpoint — steep but incredible
Bring reef shoes for rocky beach entries

name: Laem Sor Pagoda
subtext: Golden pagoda on a deserted beach — Samui's most photogenic spot
about: At the quiet southern tip of Koh Samui, a gleaming golden pagoda sits right on the beach with zero crowds. No entrance fee, no tourist buses — just you, the pagoda, and the gulf. One of Samui's most underrated and photogenic spots.
notes: Virtually empty even in peak season — Samui's best-kept secret
Golden hour photos here are magazine-worthy
Combine with a visit to the nearby Laem Sor temple
Road down is narrow — drive carefully on a scooter

name: Hushh Beach Club
subtext: New luxury beach club on Chaweng — Samui's hottest opening
about: Opened in late 2025, Hushh is Samui's newest premium beach club on Chaweng Noi. Massive pool, daybed pods, Mediterranean menu, and international DJs every weekend. Already giving Nikki Beach serious competition.
notes: Brand new — Instagram hasn't caught up yet, go before it does
Daybed pods from 2,000 THB minimum spend
Saturday DJ sessions are the best night
2,500-5,000 THB budget per person

name: The Jungle Club
subtext: Infinity pool in the treetops — best viewpoint on Samui
about: A restaurant and pool bar perched high in the Chaweng hills with a stunning infinity pool overlooking the entire east coast of Samui. The jungle setting, panoramic views, and chilled vibes make this the island's most unique hangout.
notes: Road up is STEEP — only experienced scooter riders should attempt
Free shuttle from the main road if you call ahead
Pool use included with food/drink order
Sunset from the infinity pool is peak Samui

name: Full Moon Party
subtext: The original — 10,000+ people on Haad Rin Beach
about: The world's most famous beach party happens every full moon on Haad Rin beach. Neon body paint, fire jump ropes, booming sound systems, bucket drinks, and a sea of backpackers dancing until sunrise. Love it or hate it, there's nothing else like it.
notes: Wear shoes you don't care about — broken glass on the beach
Bucket drinks are strong — pace yourself or you won't last till sunrise
Keep phone and cash in a waterproof pouch
Book accommodation WEEKS ahead — everything near Haad Rin sells out

name: Bottle Beach
subtext: Hidden paradise — only reachable by boat or jungle hike
about: Arguably the most beautiful beach on Koh Phangan, Bottle Beach is a crescent of soft sand backed by jungle and accessible only by longtail boat or a challenging 45-minute jungle hike. The isolation is the whole point.
notes: Longtail from Chaloklum — 150 THB one-way, 15 minutes
The jungle hike is beautiful but steep and slippery after rain
Two basic restaurants on the beach — bring snacks for the day
Stay overnight in the basic bungalows for the full Robinson Crusoe experience

name: Amsterdam Bar
subtext: Iconic treehouse bar in the jungle canopy
about: Built into and around massive trees in the jungle above Haad Rin, Amsterdam Bar is a legendary multi-level treehouse bar. Wooden platforms connected by bridges, fairy lights, DJs spinning till sunrise, and views over the jungle canopy.
notes: Walk uphill from Haad Rin — follow the bass and fairy lights
Multiple levels — keep climbing for the best views
Busiest on Full Moon Party and Half Moon nights
Bring mosquito repellent — it's literally in the jungle

name: Than Sadet Waterfall
subtext: Royal waterfall in the jungle — King Rama V's favorite spot
about: A beautiful multi-tiered waterfall in Koh Phangan's interior that was so loved by King Rama V he visited 14 times and carved his initials into the rocks. The jungle hike to reach it passes through pristine rainforest with natural swimming pools.
notes: 100 THB national park entry fee
Multiple tiers — Tier 3 has the best swimming pool
Road there requires a 4x4 or experienced scooter skills
King Rama V's initials are carved into rocks at the lower tier

name: Phantip Night Market
subtext: Best street food on the island — local and cheap
about: Thong Sala's nightly food market is where islanders and budget travelers eat. Thai street food classics, fresh rotis, smoothies, and some of the cheapest meals on Koh Phangan. Way better value than the beach restaurants.
notes: Best pad thai on the island — 60 THB from the corner stall
Mango sticky rice in season is heavenly
Several vegetarian and vegan stalls near the entrance
Hit it before 6 PM when the best stalls start running out

name: Secret Beach
subtext: Tiny cove through a cave entrance — feels like a movie set
about: A tiny hidden beach accessed by squeezing through a gap in the rocks on the northwest coast. Inside, a perfect little cove with turquoise water, overhanging cliffs, and complete seclusion. Feels like discovering your own private beach.
notes: Only accessible at low to mid tide — check tide charts
Entrance is through a narrow gap in the rocks — look for the rope
Bring water shoes — rocks are sharp
Don't leave valuables on the beach — nothing is secure here

name: Koh Ma Island
subtext: Walk across the ocean to a tiny island — only at low tide
about: A small island connected to Koh Phangan's northwest tip by a sandbar that appears at low tide. Walk across the ocean to reach pristine snorkeling spots, quiet beaches, and one of the best sunset viewpoints on the island.
notes: Walk across the sandbar at low tide — knee-deep at most
Best snorkeling on the north side of Koh Ma
Sunset from the sandbar is iconic — bring a camera
Don't get stranded — check when the tide comes back in
